response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY AT LEAST TWICE A DAY WITH A SOFT-BRISTLED TOOTHBRUSH.  
RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL TO REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES.  
*FLOSS CAREFULLY*  
FLOSS AROUND THE AFFECTED TOOTH TO REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES.  
*TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS*  
USE OVER-THE-COUNTER TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS TO FILL THE VOID.  
*PAIN MANAGEMENT*  
USE OVER-THE-COUNTER PAIN RELIEVERS LIKE IBUPROFEN OR ACETAMINOPHEN.  
*SAWTOOTH RINSE*  
RINSE YOUR MOUTH WITH A SALTWATER SOLUTION TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION.  
*AVOID EXTREME FOODS*  
STAY AWAY FROM HARD, STICKY, OR CHEWY FOODS.  
*MONITOR FOR CHANGES*  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR ANY CHANGES OR SIGNS OF INFECTION.  
*SEE DENTAL CARE*  
LOOK FOR DENTAL CARE OPTIONS IN YOUR CURRENT LOCATION.  
*DEAL WITH DENTAL EMERGECIES*  
SEEK DENTAL TREATMENT IMMEDIATELY IF YOU DEVELOP AN ABSCESS.  
*BOX AND CAR*  
BOX AND CAR ARE IMPORTANT FOR TEMPORARY SOLUTIONS.  
*TRADITION*  
TRADITION SUPPORTS GOOD ORAL HYGIENE PRACTICES.  
*TWO*  
TWO ARE CRITICAL FOR PROPER TREATMENT AND RECOVERY.
